data.description: The Board of Directors of The San-in Godo Bank,Ltd. has authorized a buyback plan on November 13, 2025.

data.title: Perennial Reportedly Puts Former Mediacorp Site on Sale for $350 Million
data.description:
Real estate and healthcare company Perennial Holdings (Perennial Holdings Private Limited) has put up for sale, at a guide price of more than $350 million, the former Caldecott Broadcast Centre site it had acquired from national broadcaster Mediacorp. An entity jointly owned by Perennial and Wilmar co-founder Kuok Khoon Hong had bought the hilltop site for $280.9 million in late 2020 to build what was to be the biggest cluster of leasehold Good Class Bungalows (GCBs) in a predominantly freehold GCB area in Caldecott Hill. A Perennial spokesperson said on Nov.

11 that it had "originally planned to redevelop the site to build large GCBs". Zoned for civic and community institution use under the 2019 Master Plan, the massive 752,014 sq ft leasehold land located near MacRitchie Reservoir Park and Caldecott MRT station could be redeveloped into more than 60 two-storey bungalows -- each with a minimum land area of 800 sq m, subject to approvals. Put on the market via Perennial's indirect associate firm, the site's proposed sale comes as the company recalibrates its resources to build its healthcare and healthcare real estate businesses.

Savills Singapore and Delasa were appointed real estate advisers to sell the former Caldecott Broadcast Centre land via an expressions-of-interest exercise, which will close on Jan. 15, 2026.

The San-in Godo Bank,Ltd. announces an Equity Buyback for 1,000,000 shares, representing 0.66% for ¥1,000 million.
data.description:
The San-in Godo Bank,Ltd. (TSE:8381) announces a share repurchase program. Under the program, the company will repurchase 1,000,000 shares, representing 0.66% of the outstanding shares for ¥1,000 million. The purpose of the program is to enhance return of profits to shareholders, and further increase the corporate value of the Bank by improving capital efficiency.

The program will run until January 30, 2026. As of September 30, 2025, the company had 152,496,958 shares outstanding and 4,480,514 shares in treasury.

data.title: Telenor Consolidates IoT Operations to Launch Global IoT Powerhouse
data.description:
Telenor announced a significant step in its Internet of Things (IoT) strategy by consolidating its Nordic IoT operations under Telenor Connexion, creating a unified global IoT powerhouse. This move strengthens Telenor's position as a leading provider of managed IoT solutions, enabling customers to benefit from a more streamlined, scalable, and innovative service offering. The global managed IoT market outside China is projected to reach around USD 30 billion by 2030, fuelled by rising demand for connected devices across industries including automotive, manufacturing, and smart cities.

By consolidating its IoT operations in the Nordics, Telenor IoT aims to strengthen its number-one position in the region and accelerate growth, while responding to evolving market dynamics. As part of the consolidation, Telenor will transfer its managed IoT operations from the Nordic markets to its specialised IoT unit, Telenor Connexions, bringing millions of SIMs and significant recurring revenues into the unit. Telenor ASA IoT will establish two new legal entities in Finland and Norway, strengthening the Nordic organisation, enhancing local presence, and providing even better support for customers.

data.title: India's Tata Motors posts quarterly loss on one-off impairment charge
data.description:
(Reuters) -India's Tata Motors, the country's top commercial vehicles maker, reported a quarterly loss on Thursday, as it took a one-time impairment charge related to an investment in one of its units.

The maker of Ace mini-truck reported a loss of 1.02 billion rupees ($11.6 million) for the quarter ended September 30, compared with a profit of 6.43 billion rupees a year ago.

The company split from the passenger vehicles business in October, with both businesses now operating independently under the Tata Motors group.

($1 = 87.8950 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Kashish Tandon in Bengaluru; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

Pfizer to Sell BioNTech Stake, Sources Tell Bloomberg
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller


--Pfizer is trying to sell its stake in BioNTech SE, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

--Pfizer is offering 4.55 million American depositary receipts for $108 to $111.70 a share, according to the report.


The full article: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-11-13/pfizer-looks-to-sell-stake-in-pandemic-vaccine-partner-biontech


Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 0645ET

data.title: IEA's Bosoni: 'Global Oil Supply Continues to Rise'
data.description:
Toril Bosoni, head of oil markets at the International Energy Agency, speaks on Bloomberg Television after the IEA increased its estimates for a record global oil surplus next year for a sixth consecutive month, as OPEC+ continues to revive supplies and demand growth remains subdued.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires
